name: Masaledar Macchi- Spicy Grilled Fish - India
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 lbs fish (small salmon, trout, or whole)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean and dry the fish.
2. Cut 3-4 deep diagonal slits across both sides of the fish.
3. Rub the fish with half a teaspoon of salt and one tablespoon of lemon juice. Set aside.
4. In a blender, combine remaining lemon juice, half a teaspoon of salt, onion, garlic, ginger, coriander leaves, turmeric, Garam Masala, and chili powder. Blend into a smooth paste.
5. Pour the paste into a shallow dish large enough to hold the fish. Add coconut milk; mix well. Let stand for 5-10 minutes.
6. Preheat the grill and oil the grilling rack with vegetable oil. Place the rack about 6 inches from the source of heat.
7. Remove the fish from the marinade and place it on or in the rack.
8. Grill for about 25 minutes, turning the fish every 5 minutes and basting frequently with the marinade. If using a hinged rack, turn only once. If the fish is browning too fast, move it further from the heat. Do not baste towards the end to allow the fish to form a crust.
